target_meaning: the last minute; the eleventh hour

source_meaning: to remain indifferent to, to dismiss or disregard [ allative partitive]
target_meaning: ignore, disregard

source_meaning: to beat someone at their own game
target_meaning: be outdone by

source_meaning: quietly, without much fanfare (of doing some action as quietly as possible without making any unnecessary noise, especially when to do something surreptitiously)
target_meaning: to do without being seen (by); to do behind (someone's) back; to do without the knowledge of (e.g. one's parents)

source_meaning: to dodge a bullet
target_meaning: to dodge (an attack, question, etc.), to sidestep, to evade

source_meaning: To explain with very simple terms.
target_meaning: for simplicity; for the sake of simplicity

source_meaning: on the wrong track
target_meaning: unable to get along

source_meaning: with one voice, in unison, together, simultaneously (of voices, uttered in the same time)
target_meaning: say unanimously, for two or more people to say the same thing

source_meaning: to get it, get the hint, take a hint, get the message (correctly infer a meaning that is not stated explicitly)
target_meaning: be quick on the uptake, understand the whole situation from just a few clues
